symbols.name: EventHandler
symbols.detail: [USED] Type alias used locally in Map<string, EventHandler[]> and method parameters of SpinEventEmitter | [UNIQUE] No RAG data available | [OK] Type alias is correct and sufficiently general for the pub-sub use case. | [LEAN] Minimal type alias; avoids repeating the signature at each `on`/`off` call site. | [GOOD] Type alias with no runtime behavior — no tests required. | [UNDOCUMENTED] No JSDoc comment. The variadic `unknown[]` signature is not self-evident; a brief description of its role as the listener callback shape would help consumers.

symbols.name: SpinEventEmitter
symbols.detail: [USED] Exported class with 1 runtime importer in src/engine.ts | [UNIQUE] No RAG data available | [OK] on/off/emit logic is correct: handler registration, removal by reference equality, and ordered dispatch are all sound. | [LEAN] ADR-004 explicitly justifies the custom implementation over Node.js `EventEmitter` for environment-agnosticism (browser, Deno, edge). Implementation is ~20 lines covering exactly on/off/emit with no unnecessary generics or inheritance. Single importer is noted but the design decision is documented. | [NONE] No test file exists. Core event emitter used by src/engine.ts; on/off/emit methods and edge cases (duplicate handlers, removing non-existent handler, emitting with no listeners, multiple args) are entirely untested. | [UNDOCUMENTED] Class and all three public methods (on, off, emit) lack JSDoc. Parameters, return values, and the critical lifetime/scoping constraint (each instance is independent) are undocumented.

symbols.name: SPIN_DONE
symbols.detail: [USED] Exported constant with 1 runtime importer in src/engine.ts | [UNIQUE] No RAG data available | [OK] String constant exported correctly; matches documented usage in ADR-004. | [LEAN] Named constant prevents string-literal typos in consumer code; ADR-004 documents this intent explicitly. | [NONE] No test file exists. Constant used by src/engine.ts as an event key; its correct string value is never asserted in any test. | [UNDOCUMENTED] No JSDoc. The event name string and the shape of arguments passed when it is emitted (a SpinResult) are not documented for consumers.

actions.description: Add JSDoc documentation for exported symbol: `SpinEventEmitter`
Add JSDoc documentation for exported symbol: `SPIN_DONE`

file_level.general_notes: Best practices score: 8.5/10 (0 FAIL, 4 WARN, 13 PASS)

rules.detail: tsconfig not visible from this file; code is strict-compatible — explicit return types, unknown[] instead of any[], no implicit any.
Uses unknown[] throughout EventHandler and emit signatures. No explicit or implicit any.
No union types or type assertions present.
Simple event emitter; no applicable utility type opportunities missed.
listeners field missing readonly modifier. readonly prevents accidental field reassignment while Map contents remain mutable as required.
type alias for a function signature is the correct convention over interface.
No obvious violations. Consistent formatting, no unused variables, ?? used correctly.
SpinEventEmitter (with 3 public methods) and SPIN_DONE export no JSDoc. ADR-004 documents intent but does not substitute for in-code documentation.
Uses ??, Map, for...of, unknown. No deprecated APIs.
Zero imports — zero-dependency by design per ADR-004.
emit() iterates handlers with no try-catch. A throwing handler aborts the loop, silently skipping all subsequent registered handlers — a reliability hazard in multi-listener scenarios.
No hardcoded secrets, eval, or injection vectors. Event string keys are developer-controlled named constants.
filter() in off() allocates a new array per call — acceptable for an event emitter. No N+1 or sync I/O.
Fully instantiable with new, no hidden globals, all methods directly exercisable in unit tests.
No clear missed opportunities for satisfies or const type params. Symbol.dispose would be useful (see suggestions) but absence is not a violation.

on() returns void; the modern pub-sub convention returns an unsubscribe function. ADR-004 (.anatoly/docs/02-Architecture/04-Design-Decisions.md) explicitly flags listener lifetime management as a known pain point — returning () => this.off(event, handler) resolves it without requiring callers to retain handler references.

suggestions.description: Add readonly to listeners to prevent field reassignment
suggestions.before: private listeners: Map<string, EventHandler[]> = new Map();
suggestions.after: private readonly listeners: Map<string, EventHandler[]> = new Map();

suggestions.description: Wrap each handler dispatch in try-catch so one throwing handler does not abort remaining handlers
Return an unsubscribe function from on() — directly addresses the listener lifetime concern in ADR-004
Implement Symbol.dispose to enable using-statement cleanup (TS 5.2+)
